title: Manti Turkish Dumplings Yogurt

seo.title: Turkish Manti Recipe: Authentic Dumplings with Yogurt Sauce
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Turkish Manti! These tiny, flavorful dumplings are served with a rich garlic yogurt sauce and spiced butter. Get our easy, step-by-step recipe.
longTailKeywords: how to make Turkish Manti dumplings
Turkish Manti recipe step by step
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Manti Recipe with Garlic Yogurt Sauce
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Turkish Manti
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Turkish Manti dumplings topped with garlic yogurt sauce, spiced butter, and mint.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the taste of Turkey with homemade Manti – delicate dumplings swimming in a creamy garlic yogurt sauce and drizzled with spicy butter.

description: Manti are delicate Turkish dumplings, hand-folded and filled with a savory spiced ground meat mixture. They are traditionally served with a rich, garlicky yogurt sauce and a vibrant drizzle of melted butter infused with red pepper flakes and dried mint, creating a symphony of textures and flavors in every bite. This dish is a true labor of love, perfect for special occasions or a comforting family meal.

ingredients.notes: for yogurt sauce, or to taste

instructions.title: Prepare the Manti Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the flour and 1 teaspoon of salt. Make a well in the center and crack in the eggs. Gradually add the water, mixing with your hands until a firm dough forms. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover with plastic wrap and let it rest for at least 30 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Make the Flavorful Filling
instructions.text: While the dough rests, prepare the filling. In a medium bowl, combine the ground meat, finely grated onion, 1 teaspoon salt, black pepper, and 1 teaspoon red pepper flakes. Mix thoroughly with your hands until all ingredients are well combined. Do not overmix.

instructions.title: Roll and Cut the Dough
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into 2-3 equal portions. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one portion of dough very thinly, about 1/16-inch thick, into a large rectangle. Using a knife or pizza cutter, cut the dough into small squares, approximately 1-inch by 1-inch. Repeat with the remaining dough portions.

instructions.title: Fill and Fold the Dumplings
instructions.text: Place a tiny amount of the meat filling (about 1/4 teaspoon) in the center of each dough square. To fold, pinch the opposite corners of the square together in the middle, then bring the remaining two corners to meet them, creating a small, sealed pouch. Ensure they are tightly sealed to prevent the filling from escaping during cooking. Place the folded manti on a lightly floured tray.

instructions.title: Cook the Manti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully add the manti to the boiling water, stirring gently to prevent sticking. Cook for 8-12 minutes, or until the manti float to the surface and the dough is tender. Drain well.

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Yogurt Sauce
instructions.text: While the manti cook, prepare the yogurt sauce. In a bowl, combine the plain yogurt, minced garlic, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Stir until well combined. Taste and adjust salt if necessary.

instructions.title: Make the Spiced Butter Drizzle
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat. Once melted, add 1 tablespoon of red pepper flakes and dried mint. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices. Remove from heat.

instructions.title: Serve Your Authentic Manti
instructions.text: To serve, divide the hot, drained manti among serving bowls. Spoon a generous amount of the garlic yogurt sauce over the manti. Finally, drizzle the hot spiced butter mixture over the yogurt. Garnish with additional dried mint or fresh mint leaves if desired. Serve immediately and enjoy!

tips: The key to perfect manti dough is to knead it thoroughly until it is smooth and firm, but not too stiff. It should be pliable enough to roll very thin.
Do not overfill your manti dumplings. A small amount of filling is sufficient, as too much will make them difficult to seal and prone to bursting during cooking.
For the best flavor, use good quality, full-fat plain yogurt for the sauce. Strained yogurt or Greek yogurt provides a wonderfully thick and creamy texture that holds up well with the warm dumplings.
article:
Step into the heart of Turkish cuisine, and you’ll inevitably encounter a dish that is both a labor of love and a symphony of flavors: Manti. These delicate, tiny dumplings, often described as Turkish pasta and noodles, are a culinary masterpiece. Each bite offers a burst of savory meat filling, enveloped in tender dough, all harmoniously balanced by cool, garlicky yogurt and a vibrant, spiced butter sauce. If you’re ready to embark on a delightful culinary adventure, mastering the art of the Turkish Manti Recipe is a journey worth taking.

## What is Turkish Manti?
Turkish Manti are small, boiled dumplings typically filled with a mixture of seasoned ground beef or lamb. What sets them apart from many other dumplings worldwide is their characteristic diminutive size – especially the famous Kayseri Manti, which are so small that forty can fit on a single spoon! This meticulous craftsmanship is a testament to the care and tradition embedded in their preparation. While the term "Turkish pasta and noodles" might initially conjure images of spaghetti or tagliatelle, Manti are indeed a form of pasta, miniature pockets of dough that are boiled and served with an array of delectable toppings. The traditional serving involves draining the cooked Manti, topping them generously with a cool, garlicky yogurt sauce, and then drizzling a hot, aromatic butter sauce infused with red pepper flakes and dried mint. Sometimes a sprinkle of sumac or additional red pepper flakes completes this iconic dish, making it a feast for both the eyes and the palate. Manti is more than just food; it’s a dish often prepared for special occasions, family gatherings, and celebrations, embodying warmth, hospitality, and culinary heritage.

## Ingredients You Need for Manti

To create your very own authentic Turkish Manti Recipe, gather these essential ingredients:

For the Dough:
*   3 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 cup water (or more, as needed)
*   1 teaspoon salt

For the Filling:
*   1 lb ground beef or lamb (preferably lean)
*   1 small onion, very finely grated or minced
*   1 teaspoon salt
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes (optional, for a little kick)
*   2 tablespoons fresh parsley, finely chopped (optional)

For the Yogurt Sauce:
*   2 cups plain full-fat yogurt (Greek yogurt works well for its thickness)
*   2-3 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/2 teaspoon salt

For the Spiced Butter Sauce (Red Sauce):
*   4 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 teaspoon red pepper flakes (pul biber is traditional)
*   1 teaspoon dried mint (nane)
*   1/2 teaspoon sweet paprika

Optional Garnish:
*   Sumac
*   Fresh mint or parsley leaves

## Step-by-Step Manti Instructions

Here’s how to bring this delightful Turkish Manti Recipe to life in your kitchen:

1.  **Prepare the Dough:** In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt. Create a well in the center and add the egg and water. Gradually mix the wet ingredients into the flour until a shaggy dough forms. Turn it out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover the dough with a damp cloth or plastic wrap and let it rest for at least 30 minutes at room temperature. This resting period is crucial for a pliable dough.

2.  **Make the Filling:** While the dough rests, prepare the filling. In a medium bowl, combine the ground meat, grated onion, salt, black pepper, red pepper flakes (if using), and chopped parsley (if using). Mix thoroughly until all ingredients are well combined. Do not overmix.

3.  **Roll and Cut the Dough:** Divide the rested dough into 3-4 equal portions. Take one portion and roll it out on a lightly floured surface into a very thin sheet, about 1-2 mm thick. The thinner the dough, the more delicate your Manti will be. Using a sharp knife or a pizza cutter, cut the dough into small squares, approximately 1-inch by 1-inch (2.5 cm x 2.5 cm). This size is key for traditional Manti.

4.  **Fill and Fold the Manti:** Place a tiny amount (about 1/4 teaspoon) of the meat filling in the center of each dough square. To fold, pinch two opposite corners of the square together, then bring the remaining two corners up and pinch them together at the top, forming a small, open-top pouch or a boat shape. Alternatively, you can pinch all four corners together to form a small purse. Ensure they are well-sealed to prevent the filling from escaping during cooking. Repeat this process with the remaining dough and filling. You can dust the finished Manti lightly with flour and place them on a baking sheet to prevent sticking.

5.  **Cook the Manti:** Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully drop the Manti into the boiling water, stirring gently to prevent them from sticking together. Cook for 5-8 minutes, or until the Manti float to the surface and the dough is tender.

6.  **Prepare the Sauces:** While the Manti cook, prepare your sauces. For the yogurt sauce, combine the yogurt, minced garlic, and salt in a small bowl. Stir well. For the spiced butter sauce, melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Once melted, add the red pepper flakes, dried mint, and paprika. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices.

7.  **Assemble and Serve:** Using a slotted spoon, transfer the cooked Manti directly from the pot to individual serving plates. Spoon a generous amount of the garlicky yogurt sauce over the hot Manti. Finally, drizzle the vibrant spiced butter sauce over the yogurt and Manti. Garnish with a sprinkle of sumac or fresh mint/parsley if desired. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Turkish Manti

*   **Dough Consistency is Key:** Your Manti dough should be firm but elastic, not too sticky. If it’s too wet, add a little more flour; if too dry, a tiny bit more water. The resting period makes it much easier to roll thinly.
*   **Roll It Thin:** The hallmark of authentic Manti is its delicate, thin dough. Don't be afraid to roll the dough until it's almost translucent.
*   **Small is Beautiful:** The smaller the Manti, the more authentic and delightful they are. While it's time-consuming, the effort truly pays off in the final texture and taste.
*   **Seal Them Well:** Ensure all the edges are firmly pinched together to prevent the meat filling from escaping into the boiling water.
*   **Don't Overfill:** A tiny dollop of filling is all you need for these small dumplings. Too much will make them hard to seal and prone to breaking.
*   **Batch Cooking & Freezing:** Manti can be made in large batches. Once folded, arrange raw Manti on a floured baking sheet in a single layer and freeze until solid. Then transfer them to a freezer-safe bag. Cook from frozen, adding a couple of extra minutes to the cooking time.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** Fresh, lean ground meat and full-fat, creamy yogurt will significantly enhance the flavor of your Manti.

## Serving Suggestions for Manti

Turkish Manti, with its rich flavors and satisfying textures, is typically served as a substantial main course. It's a complete meal in itself, especially given the protein from the meat and yogurt. To complement its richness, a simple side salad, like a refreshing Shepherd's Salad (Çoban Salata) with tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions, makes an excellent accompaniment. A glass of chilled Ayran, the traditional Turkish yogurt drink, is also a perfect pairing, cutting through the richness of the butter sauce and cleansing the palate. Don't forget some crusty bread to sop up any leftover sauces!

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Turkish Manti Recipe is truly divine, there are several ways to adapt it to different tastes or dietary needs:

*   **Vegetarian Manti:** For a meat-free version, fill your Manti with a spiced mixture of lentils, mashed potatoes, finely chopped spinach and feta cheese, or even mushrooms.
*   **Alternative Meats:** While less traditional, some recipes call for ground chicken or turkey for a lighter filling. Ensure the meat is well-seasoned to maintain flavor.
*   **Sauce Enhancements:** Some regional variations of the spiced butter sauce include a touch of tomato paste for a deeper, richer flavor. You can also experiment with different herbs like fresh dill or thyme in the yogurt sauce.
*   **Ready-Made Dough:** If time is a constraint, you can use store-bought wonton wrappers or even thin pasta sheets, though the texture of homemade dough is undeniably superior and part of the Manti experience.
*   **Baked Manti:** While most Manti are boiled, some regional types, like "Yağlama" (though technically a different dish, it shares some Manti characteristics), are baked in layers with meat and yogurt, offering a different texture.

Embarking on the journey of making Turkish Manti from scratch is a rewarding culinary experience. It’s a dish that embodies the spirit of Turkish home cooking – a labor of love that results in an incredibly satisfying and flavorful meal. So, roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to delight your senses with this authentic Turkish Manti Recipe. The effort is absolutely worth it for the taste of these wonderful Turkish dumplings.
